reference: There could be many reasons for the pain, such as a cyst, an old injury, synovial bursitis, and what I suspect is a hamstring strain. However, without being evaluated, I cannot be sure of that, so you need to see a physiotherapist or orthopedic doctor. Until then, give your leg rest and do not try to strain that part by repeatedly doing the same movement, such as cross sitting. Also, using a knee cap or a crepe bandage can help to some extent while walking. I hope you find this useful. If you need any help, you can revert back.
text: There could be many reasons for the pain, such as a cyst, an old injury, synovial bursitis, and what I suspect is a hamstring strain. However, without being evaluated, I cannot be sure of that. So, you need to see a physiotherapist or orthopedic doctor. Until then, give your leg rest and do not try to strain that part. Heatedly doing the same movement, such as cross sitting. Also, using a knee cap or a crepe bandage can help to some extent while walking. I hope you find this useful. If you need any help, you can revert back.
